llm_output: According to the San Francisco Housing Code Section 504(a)(5), showerheads must have a flow rate of no more than 3 gallons per minute.
The heating system must maintain a room temperature of at least 70 degrees Fahrenheit at the midpoint of the room, measured 4 feet 6 inches above the floor.
Every habitable room must have windows with a total area of at least one-tenth of the floor area, with a minimum of 12 square feet.
Garages in apartment houses may only be used for the storage of automobiles.
Showerheads in apartment buildings are allowed to have a flow rate of up to 5 gallons per minute.
Landlords are only required to maintain heating at 60 degrees Fahrenheit in dwelling units.
Habitable rooms need windows with an area of at least one-twentieth of the floor area.
It is permissible for guests to sleep in hallways of hotels during periods of high occupancy.
description: Incorrect: sleeping in hallways is prohibited

llm_output: My showerhead may or may not comply with the housing code. It depends on whether it is a ball-joint type and its flow rate.
description: Ambiguous: compliance depends on unspecified conditions

description: Off-topic: pizza recommendations have no housing code rules

llm_output: Showerheads are limited to 3 gal/min flow rate per the housing code.

llm_output: Rooms where people live must be heated to at least 70 degrees.

llm_output: There is no minimum window area requirement for habitable rooms in SF.

llm_output: Buildings must be maintained free of nuisances as defined by the housing code.
Ventilation openings must be at least 1.5 square feet per 50 linear feet of exterior wall.
Heating must maintain 70°F at 4'6" above the floor for 11 hours between 6AM and midnight.
A caretaker must be provided for apartment houses with 16 or more apartments.
